title: UX Teardown of a South African Website
tagline: Critique a real SA site's booking flow and propose three fixes.
scenario: You have been asked to review the booking or checkout flow of any well-known South African website of your choice (examples: Takealot, Checkers Sixty60, Uber Eats, Discovery, SARS eFiling, Computicket). Pick one, complete a realistic task on it, and report back.
brief: Complete the task with a screen recorder or just detailed notes. You do not need to share the recording. Document what you noticed and what you would change.
deliverables: The site and specific task you attempted (one line)
Three specific UX issues you encountered, each with a concrete proposed fix
One thing the site does well that you would keep
Your method for deciding priority if the product team could only ship one of your fixes
submissionGuidance: Vague issues like "confusing layout" do not count. Be specific: which screen, which button, what you expected, what happened.

rubric.label: Observation Quality
rubric.description: Are the three issues specific, real, and well-described?

rubric.label: Proposed Fixes
rubric.description: Are the fixes practical, proportionate, and targeted at the root issue?

rubric.label: Balanced View
rubric.description: Is the "keep" item thoughtful, not filler?
